data.title: HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital prices offering of $300M notes
data.description:
• HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (NYSE:HASI) has priced its private offering of $300M of 6.375% green senior unsecured notes due 2034.
  • The notes will be additional notes and form part of the same class as the company’s existing 6.375% green senior unsecured notes due 2034.
  • Net proceeds from the offering will be approximately $297M that will be used to temporarily repay a portion of the outstanding borrowings under the company’s unsecured credit facility and its commercial paper program.
  • The company will use cash equal to the net proceeds from the offering to acquire, invest in or refinance, in whole or in part, new and/or existing eligible green projects.

data.title: AutoZone misses estimates with its FQ1 report, but points to improving trends
data.description:
AutoZone (NYSE:AZO) is on watch after falling short of estimates with its Q3 earnings report. Domestic same-store sales were up 0.3% during the quarter vs. +0.7% consensus, and international same-store sales were up 1.0% vs 1.02% consensus. Total revenue was up 1.9% year-over-year to $4.28 billion, but missed the consensus estimate by $30 million.

Operating profit fell 0.9% during the quarter to $841.1 million. Net income for the quarter was $564.9 million compared to $593.5 million in the same period last year, while diluted earnings per share were $32.52 vs. $33.64 consensus and the $32.55 mark from a year ago.

Under its share repurchase program, AutoZone (AZO) repurchased 160K shares of its common stock during the first quarter, at an average price per share of $3,156, for a total investment of $505.2 million.

During the quarter ended November 23, 2024, AutoZone (AZO) opened 23 new stores in the U.S., six in Mexico and five in Brazil for a total of 34 new stores. As of November 23, the company had 6,455 stores in the U.S., 800 in Mexico and 132 in Brazil for a total store count of 7,387.

CEO Phil Daniele pointed to progress in DIY same store sales result from the prior quarter as average ticket and traffic trends improved. "Our domestic Commercial sales were up 3.2% and we were encouraged by the improving trends seen at the end of the quarter," he noted. Daniele said currency rate moves depressed reported international sales and earnings growth.

Looking ahead, AutoZone (AZO) said it is well positioned for growth heading into the remainder of the fiscal year. "As we continue to invest in our business, we remain committed to our disciplined approach of a focus on increasing earnings and operating cash flow, all while delivering strong shareholder value," highlighted Daniele.

Shares of AutoZone (AZO) tricked 0.1% higher in premarket action on Tuesday. The auto retail stock is up almost 29% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: AutoZone FQ1 misses top-line and bottom-line estimates
data.description: *Other Operating Data Consensus Source: Bloomberg

data.title: Interactive Strength regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• Interactive Strength (NASDAQ:TRNR) said it has received a letter from Nasdaq, stating that the firm has regained compliance with the minimum bid price rule.
  • The company said it has also regained compliance with the requirement of having more than 500K publicly held shares.

data.title: Mobilicom secures production orders from U.S. customer, stock gains
data.description:
• Mobilicom (NASDAQ:MOB) has secured its latest initial production-scale orders for its cybersecure SkyHopper Pro datalinks from a U.S.-based drone manufacturer that serves commercial and defense customers globally, it said on Tuesday.
  • MOB stock rose +15.83% premarket to $2.78.
  • SkyHopper Pro has been integrated into high payload long range drones that are being sold to defense and commercial end users across the U.S.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Canadian Solar's unit signs 10-year power purchase agreement
data.description:
• Canadian Solar's (NASDAQ:CSIQ) unit, Recurrent Energy said it has signed a 10-year power purchase agreement with a major U.S.-based technology company.
  • Under the agreement, the firm will get renewable energy from a Recurrent Energy solar PV project located in Valladolid.
  • The project has an installed capacity of 300 MWp and is expected to be operational by 2026.

data.title: G-III Apparel Non-GAAP EPS of $2.59 beats by $0.31, revenue of $1.09B misses by $10M
data.description:
• G-III Apparel press release (NASDAQ:GIII): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.59 beats by $0.31.
  • Revenue of $1.09B (+1.9% Y/Y) misses by $10M.

  • Fiscal 2025

    Net sales are expected to increase by approximately 2% to approximately $3.15 billion (prior approximately $3.20 billion), compared to net sales of $3.10 billion for fiscal 2024.

    Net income is expected to be between $185.0 million and $190.0 million (prior $179.0 million and $184.0 million), or diluted earnings per share between $4.08 and $4.18 (prior $3.94 and $4.04). This compares to net income of $176.2 million, or $3.75 per diluted share, for fiscal 2024.

    Non-GAAP net income for fiscal 2025 is expected to be between $186.0 million and $191.0 million (prior $180.0 and $185.0 million), or diluted earnings per share between $4.10 and $4.20 (prior $3.95 and $4.05). This compares to non-GAAP net income of $189.8 million, or diluted earnings per share of $4.04 for fiscal 2024.

    Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2025 is expected to be between $309.0 million and $314.0 million (prior $305.0 million and $310.0 million) compared to adjusted EBITDA of $324.1 million in fiscal 2024.

    Net interest expense is expected to be approximately $20.4 million, including a $1.6 million non-GAAP charge related to the write-off of deferred financing costs associated with the redemption of the Notes.

    We estimate a tax rate of 28.6% for fiscal 2025.

data.title: Rocket Lab pushes ahead with suborbital hypersonic technology initiative for the Department of Defense
data.description:
Rocket Lab USA (NASDAQ:RKLB) announced late on Tuesday that it successfully launched a suborbital mission in November to test hypersonic technology for the Department of Defense.

Hypersonic technology refers to vehicles and systems capable of traveling at speeds exceeding Mach 5, or five times the speed of sound12. These systems operate in a unique aerodynamic regime characterized by extreme temperatures, thin shock layers, and complex air flows.

The Rocket Lab (RKLB) mission provided hypersonic test launch capabilities under the Multi-Service Advanced Capability Hypersonics Test Bed project, which aims to increase hypersonic flight testing for the United States. The project was awarded by Naval Surface Warfare Center Crane through the Strategic and Spectrum Missions Advanced Resilient Trusted Systems Other Transaction Authority vehicle on behalf of the U.S. Department of Defense Test Resource Management Center.

Notably, Rocket Lab's (RKLB) test platform showcased a new suite of cutting-edge technologies optimized for hypersonic technology tests with vastly increased payloads. The company also designed, manufactured, assembled, and integrated the experimental hypersonic instrumentation which was launched on this mission, but on a highly accelerated timeline.

Shares of Rocket Lab (RKLB) traded 1.30% lower in premarket action on Tuesday.

data.title: Buckle sets $2.50 special cash dividend for January
data.description:
The Buckle (NYSE:BKE) announced on Tuesday that its board of directors authorized a $2.50 per share special cash dividend to be paid to shareholders of record at the close of business on January 15. The board also authorized a $0.35 per share quarterly dividend to be paid to shareholders of record at the close of business on January 15.

Both the $2.50 per share special cash dividend and the $0.35 per share quarterly dividend ($2.85 per share in total) are payable on January 29, and will be paid together.

Of note, The retailer matched the $2.50 special dividend paid out on Janutry 12, 2024, and did not change its dividend payout rate.

Shares of Buckle (BKE) are up 15% over the last six weeks and traded very close to the 52-week share price high.
